summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the package root containing GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) at line 6, within a `targets`/`sources` field. npm implicitly invokes `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present — even without any declared install/postinstall script — and node-gyp evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command during the configure step. This causes the embedded command to execute on every `npm install` of the package. The package presents itself as a Backstage GitLab plugin (a pure JavaScript/TypeScript frontend plugin), a category that has no legitimate need for a native addon build or a binding.gyp. The presence of a binding.gyp whose sole structural purpose is to host a command-expansion expression — rather than to build a real native module from shipped C/C++ sources — is the install-time RCE fingerprint. Provider safety filtering of the traced contents corroborates that the embedded code reads as operational malware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
